info.title: Vesta API
info.description:
## Getting started

### Authentication

The API key must be specified via `Authorization: Token <apikey>`. To try out API calls from this site, click on an endpoint in the left sidebar, and enter your API key **(with the `Token` prefix)** in the `Authorization` field.

### Versioning

The Vesta API is strongly versioned to minimize breaking changes within versions.

Every API call must specify its API version using the header `X-Api-Version` with a value of `26.2`, `26.1`, `25.4`, or `experimental`.

Stable versions are specified by a version number `YY.Q`, e.g. `26.2`. The Experimental version is specified by `experimental` and is not stable and so is subject to breaking changes at any time.

The next API release will be `26.3` which will be released in early July 2026.

| Stable version | Release date | Date stable version will be removed |
| -------------- | ------------ | -------------------------------------- |
| `26.2` | April 6, 2026 | December 4, 2026 |
| `26.1` | January 12, 2026 | September 4, 2026 |
| `25.4` | October 6, 2025 | June 5, 2026 |

#### API Change Examples
| API Change | Type |
| ---------- | ---- |
| Add new properties to a model | Non-breaking |
| Add new API endpoint	| Non-breaking |
| Add new enum members | Non-breaking but enums should be treated as potentially open-ended as these changes will go out to all versions immediately |
| Remove or rename property from a model | Breaking |
| Remove or rename enum member | Breaking |
| Change property type, such as widening the type from an int to decimal | Breaking for a response type as consumers may have the property already typed to an int and a floating point value may break them |
| Change property type, such as from a simple value like a bool or string to an object containing the value as a property | Breaking |
| Change a property to be nullable | Most properties should already be nullable but making a property nullable could be breaking for a response type if a consumer has it typed as non-nullable |
| Change API behavior | Breaking |
| Remove API endpoint | Breaking |

In general breaking changes will be versioned but may not be for bug fixes or other exceptional circumstances.

### Rate limiting

The API is rate limited. Every API response will include the headers `RateLimit-Limit`, `RateLimit-Remaining`, and `RateLimit-Reset`, with the following definitions:
- `RateLimit-Limit`: The maximum number of requests that can be made in a given time period.
- `RateLimit-Remaining`: The number of requests remaining in the current time period.
- `RateLimit-Reset`: The number of seconds after which the current time period will reset.

The rate limit is currently defaulted to 2000 requests per hour. To increase this limit, please contact Vesta.
Requests to `/api/v1/ping` are not included in this limit.
If you exceed this limit, you will receive a `429 Too Many Requests` response with the `Retry-After` header set to the number of seconds after which you can retry your request.

### Errors

Errors will generally be returned as unstructured strings with a status code between 400 and 599.

Requests will include an `X-Vesta-Request-Id` header, barring exceptional cases like the request failing at our load balancer.
If you encounter an unexpected error, please include this value when reporting it to Vesta.

### Request Context

You may optionally include an additional `X-Vesta-Request-Context` header that will be applied to audit records generated by the API request.  Potential usages are to
record the source system that generated the API request, or to include a uuid that identifies the context of the source system that made the API request.

## Loans and entities

The base object is a `Loan`. There are several entity types that are children of the loan object, but may have inter-entity relationships as well. The entity types are:

- Asset
- Borrower
- Borrower business
- Closing adjustment
- Closing cost
- Collateral exception
- Concurrent financing
- Credit inquiry
- Income
- Liability
- Non borrowing related party
- Pricing request
- Property
- Property valuation
- Proration
- Public record
- Tax transcript request form
- Underwriting exception

When calling any of the GET endpoints, inter-entity relationships will be specified as IDs (e.g. the `Income` object may include a pointer to a property via `rentalPropertyId`).
However, the `GET /loans/{loanId}` endpoint will return all children as full objects. For example, the `Loan` field `ownedProperties` will contain full `Property` objects, not just IDs.
See the endpoint details and the schema models on the left of the page for more information.

### Inter-entity relationships

There are a few different ways of linking and unlinking inter-entity relationships such as borrower assets.

When adding entities you can link them in one of two ways.

First, you can explicitly add entities directly as children of another entity like such in this `POST /loans` request.
```json
{
  "borrowers": [
    {
      "firstName": "John",
      "lastName": "Doe",
      "assets": [
        {
          "assetType": "CheckingAccount",
          "assetValue": 5346.21
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

Or you can utilize `ref` fields to link them which has the added benefit of returning the id of the added entity with the ref in the `refMappings` response like so in this `POST /loans` request.
```json
{
  "borrowers": [
    {
      "firstName": "John",
      "lastName": "Doe",
      "assetsRefs": ["checkingAccountRef"]
    }
  ],
  "assets": [
    {
      "ref": "checkingAccountRef",
      "assetType": "CheckingAccount",
      "assetValue": 5346.21
    }
  ]
}
```

When updating existing entities you can link or unlink them in one of two ways.

First, you can update the linked id(s) field. For a one-to-many or many-to-many relationship the linked ids field will be a collection of id's and so if you add an id to the collection it will then link the two entities
but if you set the id list field and exclude the related entity id, the two entities will become unlinked. For a one-to-one relationship you can just set the linked id field to the id of the entity you'd like to link or `null` if you'd like to unlink it.
Here's an example `PATCH /loans` request.
```json
{
  "borrowers": [
    {
      "id": "borrowerIdToUpdate",
      "assetsIds": [
        "assetIdToLinkToBorrower" // if there was an existing asset linked here it would be removed if not included in the collection
      ],
      "spouseId": "spouseBorrowerIdToLink"
    }
  ]
}
```

The other way to link or unlink a one-to-many or many-to-many inter-entity relationship is by updating the linked entity collection utilizing the `$action` field to `Add` or `Remove` an entity like so in this `PATCH /loans` request.
```json
{
  "borrowers": [
    {
      "id": "borrowerIdToUpdate",
      "assets": [
        {
          "$action": "Add",
          "id": "assetIdToLinkToBorrower"
        },
        {
          "$action": "Remove",
          "id": "assetIdToUnlinkFromBorrower"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}
```

### `POST` Loans and entities

When calling `POST /loans`, you can create entities along with the loan by filling out the direct loan fields (e.g. `borrowers`).

To specify inter-entity relationships, you can include a `ref` field on any entity and specify it in the relationship on other entities,
(e.g. Specify a Property with field `"ref"="MyProperty"` and a separate Income with field `"rentalPropertyRef"="MyProperty"`)

Additionally, the ID of any entity with a Ref field specified will be included in the response.
Any entity without a Ref field will still be created but its ID will not be returned.

Ref fields can be used in all loan entity `POST` and `PATCH` endpoints.

### `PATCH` Loans and entities

`PATCH` requests can be used to partially update existing loans and entities. Patches are applied to nested objects as well. For example, given the following request body to update a loan:
```json
{
  "loanPurpose": "Purchase",
  "loanProduct": {
    "mortgageType": "Conventional",
    "loanAmortizationType": null
  },
  "assets": [{
    "id": "assetToUpdateId",
    "accountIdentifier": "1234567890",
  }, {
    "id": "assetToRemoveId",
    "$action": "Remove"
  }]
}
```

The following changes will be made to the loan:

- `loanPurpose` will be set to `"Purchase"`
- `loanProduct.mortgageType` will be set to `"Conventional"`
- `loanProduct.loanAmortizationType` will be set to `null`
- No other fields on `loan` and `loanProduct` will be changed
- The `Asset` with ID `assetToUpdateId` will have its `accountIdentifier` field set to `"1234567890"`
- The `Asset` with ID `assetToRemoveId` will be removed from the loan

Some miscellaneous notes:

- Removing an entity from an inter-entity relationship does **not** remove it from the loan, but merely severs the relationship. To remove the entity entirely, either call the relevant `DELETE` endpoint or remove it from the top-level loan object.
- `PATCH` requests can also be used to add new entities to collections. For example, to add an Asset, you can add an Asset entity to the Loan collection, omitting the `id` field. If a `ref` field is specified the newly created entities ID will be returned in the response.
- If you specify an `id` field on an entity that does not exist, the request will fail.
- If the schema for a collection has an `id` field then for `PATCH` requests the items in that collection are updated by `id`, otherwise the whole collection is replaced.
- The `$action` field can be included in collections sent in `PATCH` requests. The allowed values of this field are `"Remove"` and `"Add"`. `"Remove"` will remove the specified item by `id` from the collection. If you’re removing a top-level entity from an inter-entity relationship such as `borrower.liabilities` the relationship with the borrower and the liability will be severed but the liability will still exist on the loan. However, if you’re removing a top-level entity from a loan collection such as `loan.liabilities` the entity will be removed from the loan same as calling the relevant `DELETE` entity endpoint. If you’re removing any other model from a collection such as `borrower.militaryServicePeriods` then the model will be removed from the loan. `"Add"` can be used to establish a relationship with two top-level entities such as adding an existing liability by its `id` to the `borrower.liabilities` collection.

### Custom fields and entities

All entities support custom fields.
Viewing and modifying which custom fields are available in each Process Version can be done through the Version Manager in the Admin portal.
This will include the custom field's "API identifier", which will default to the field name without special characters but can be modified.

Once custom fields are created on the process version of a loan, they can be set and retrieved on each entity through the `customFields` object on each entity.
`customFields` is a JSON object, where each key is an API identifier for a custom field, and the value of each field is determined by the type of the custom field.
All custom fields are nullable.

### Idempotency

All `GET` requests to an endpoint prefixed with `/loans/` will include an `ETag` header, like `ETag: "1234567890"`. This value can be used to make `PATCH`, and `DELETE` requests on entities idempotent by including the `If-Match` header with the same value.
For example, if you make a `PATCH` request with the `If-Match` header set to the `ETag` value from a `GET` request, the request will only succeed if the `ETag` value has not changed since the `GET` request.
If the `ETag` value has changed, the request will fail with a `412 Precondition Failed` response.
The specific format of `ETag` values is not guaranteed, and should not be relied upon.

Separately, users of the API can optionally send `Idempotency-Key` headers with `POST`, `PATCH`, and `DELETE` loan and entity requests.
When a request is made with an idempotency key that has already been used, no action will be taken, and the original (cached) response will be returned.
Replayed responses will include the header `Idempotency-Replayed: true`.
While the first request is still running a `409 Conflict` response will be returned for other requests with the same idempotency key.
Idempotency keys should be UUIDs, matching the case-insensitive regex `^[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{12}$`.
Keys are valid for one hour. If a request is made with an expired key, the request will be processed as if a new key was provided.
This behavior applies to non-transient errors as well. Transient errors will be retried.

### Impersonation

You can make api requests as a specific user by sending the `X-Vesta-User-Id` header with a value of the user's id.
This currently only restricts what endpoints are available based on the user's roles and their permissions.
It does not right now change the content returned from the endpoint. If the user does not have the permissions required then a 403 Forbidden response will be returned.

## Webhooks

Vesta supports sending authenticated HTTP requests to an API endpoint of your choosing.

### Authentication

Webhook endpoints support two mutually exclusive authentication modes:

**Token mode** (default):
- Requests include `Authorization: Token <xyz>` where `<xyz>` is the shared secret.
- Authenticate inbound requests by comparing the token to your shared secret.

**HMAC signature mode**:
- The `Authorization` header is **not sent**. Instead, requests are authenticated via HMAC-SHA256 payload signatures.
- The shared secret never travels over the wire, and the signature additionally proves payload integrity.

### Payload Signatures

When payload signing is enabled, two headers are sent with each request:

| Header | Value |
|--------|-------|
| `X-Webhook-Signature-256` | `sha256=<hex>` — lowercase hex-encoded HMAC-SHA256 digest |
| `X-Webhook-Timestamp` | Unix epoch seconds (string) when the request was signed |

The signed payload is constructed as:

```
signed_payload = <X-Webhook-Timestamp> + "." + <raw JSON body>
```

To verify a webhook request:

1. Read the `X-Webhook-Timestamp` header and the raw request body.
2. Concatenate them with a `.` separator: `timestamp + "." + body`.
3. Compute the HMAC-SHA256 of the result using your shared secret as the key.
4. Compare your hex-encoded digest (prefixed with `sha256=`) against the `X-Webhook-Signature-256` header using a constant-time comparison to prevent timing attacks.
5. Optionally, reject requests where the timestamp is too far from the current time to mitigate replay attacks.

### Webhook Types

We currently support the following webhook types:

Loan:

- Loan data changed
- Loan field decrypted
- Loan assignee changed
- Loan stage changed
- Entity relationship changed
- Loan archived
- Loan unarchived
- Loan created
- Loan underwriter decision changed
Tasks:

- Task created
- Task cancelled
- Task reopened
- Task snoozed
- Task assigned
- Task due date changed
- Task modified
- Task completed
Automated actions:

- Automated action created
- Automated action started
- Automated action cancelled
- Automated action completed
- Automated action failed
- Automated action removed
Documents:

- Document uploaded
- Document status changed
- Document reclassified
Disclosures:

- Disclosures sent
- Disclosures created
- Disclosures signed
- Disclosures consent received
- Disclosures completed
- Disclosures expired
- Disclosures link expired
Job status:

- Job status update
Notes:

- Note added
Integration calls:

- Integration request sent
- Integration response received
Objectives and related Tasks / Automated Actions:

- Objective created
- Objective status updated
- Objective deleted (by admin)
- Objective assigned
- Objective task created
- Objective task status updated
- Objective task deleted (by admin)
- Objective automated action created
- Objective automated action status updated
- Objective automated action deleted (by admin)
- Objective condition status updated
- Objective condition modified
- Objective condition created
Admin configuration:

- Process version created
- Process version renamed
- Process version deleted
- Process version status updated
- Admin config import from environment
- Process version merge started
- Process version merge completed
- Process version merge discarded
- Process version merge timed out
- User created
- User status changed
- Role created
- Role updated
- Role deleted
- Global file contact created
- Global file contact updated
- Global file contact deleted
AI agent:



You may choose which of these webhook subscription types to subscribe to, and will receive all events for each subscribed subscription type. It is up to the client to filter events for the subscription type which are not relevant to their integration.
These can be configured in Vesta admin if you have the View and edit admin page permissions for Webhook endpoits.

Webhook schema definitions are available in the left sidebar.

### Batched loan webhooks

In API version `26.2` and later, a webhook endpoint can opt in to **batching** loan-scoped webhook deliveries. When batching is enabled on the endpoint, multiple webhook events that belong to the same underlying change batch may be combined into a **single** HTTP POST instead of one request per event.

The combined payload uses the `LoanBatchWebhook` schema (`subscriptionType` is `LoanBatch`). The `events` array holds each event's payload in the same shape as a standalone delivery for that event if it were sent alone.

You still subscribe only to the specific loan webhook types you need; batching changes how eligible deliveries are grouped, not which subscription types apply.

### Retry behavior

Failed webhooks, defined as non-2xx responses or responses taking longer than 10 seconds, will be retried according to the following schedule:

- 1 minute
- 10 minutes
- 1 hour
- 24 hours
- 48 hours

Note that 3xx responses are considered unsuccessful and will also be retried.

Retry timings are best effort and should not be relied upon.

### Data webhook paths

Data changed webhooks include several fields to help indicate which field was changed:

- `entityType` is 1:1 with the entity type in the Vesta API. For example, `"entityType"="Loan"` indicates that the webhook is for a loan.
- `entityId` is the ID of the entity that was changed.
- `path` is a [JSONPath](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/id/draft-goessner-dispatch-jsonpath-00.html) expression navigating from the base entity response type to the updated field. For example,
  - Given a loan webhook, `"path"="$.loanProduct.mortgageType"` indicates that the webhook is for a change to the `"mortgageType"` field on the `"loanProduct"` field on the loan.
  - Given a loan webhook, `"path"="$.loanProduct.closingAdjustments[?(@.id=='closingAdjustmentId')].amount"` indicates that the webhook is for a change to the `"amount"` field on the `"closingAdjustment"` with ID `"closingAdjustmentId"` on the loan's loan product.

The only JSONPath filter expression currently in use is `[?(@.id=='<id>')]`, which filters a collection by the `id` field.

The granularity of field changes is not guaranteed. In particular, it's possible to receive a webhook for an object type, e.g. `borrower.currentAddress`.

### Data changed webhook encryption

Vesta optionally supports sending the updated **value** in data changed webhooks.
These values will be sent, encrypted via 256-bit AES-GCM and base-64 encoded, in the field `data`.
Encryption metadata will be base-64 encoded and sent in the `iv` and `tag` fields.
The encryption key should be 256 bits and sent to Vesta.
For now, it is the customer's responsibility to rotate keys.

To set up this feature, please contact Vesta.

Note that webhooks are not guaranteed to be in order. Therefore, to ensure that you have the most recent update for a given field,
you should call the Vesta API to retrieve the latest value instead of relying on the `data` field.

<details>
<summary style="font-size:160%;"><b>Migrating from 26.1 to 26.2</b></summary>

- Please review the breaking data model changes below.
- If you have any questions or concerns, please contact Vesta.

### Breaking changes

- Creating or patching `ClosingCost` entities to use computed fee types or computed fee categories is rejected with 400 (those fees are populated by the system instead)
- Creating `PartnerBroker` or `CorrespondentLender` loan file contacts with `IsPrimary` true is rejected with 400; assign the contact as the loan originator instead

#### Data model changes

- `Loan`
  - Removed `TitleInsuranceProvider` (use `TitleInsuranceProvider` on the subject or owned `Property` instead)
- `LoanProduct`
  - Made `RawPrice` read-only and added `RawPriceMetadata` which can be used instead to override the value
- `Property`
  - Made the following fields read-only
    - `PropertyValuationForm`
    - `AppraisalProvider`
    - `AppraisalOrderedDate`
    - `AppraisalScheduledDate`
    - `AppraisalInspectionCompletedDate`
    - `AppraisalReceivedDate`
    - `AppraisalDeliveredToBorrowerDate`
    - `ConditionOfAppraisal`
    - `PropertyValuationMethodType`
    - `PropertyValuationMethodTypeOtherDescription`





- Misc changes
  - Removed the singular `BrokerRoleType` and `CorrespondentRoleType` fields from `GlobalFileContact` and `FileContact` in favor of the `BrokerRoleTypes` and `CorrespondentRoleTypes` collections
  - Made `PrimaryContacts.PartnerBroker` and `PrimaryContacts.CorrespondentLender` read-only
  - Renamed `LoanPassGetQuotesRequest`'s `DutyToServeIndicator` field to `DutyToServeProductIndicator`





### Non-breaking changes

- Added the following loan assigned-users endpoints:
    - `POST /loans/{loanId}/assigned-users`
    - `GET /loans/{loanId}/assigned-users`
- Added the following tax transcript request form endpoints:
    - `POST /loans/{loanId}/tax-transcript-request-forms`
    - `GET /loans/{loanId}/tax-transcript-request-forms`
    - `GET /loans/{loanId}/tax-transcript-request-forms/{taxTranscriptRequestFormId}`
    - `PATCH /loans/{loanId}/tax-transcript-request-forms/{taxTranscriptRequestFormId}`
    - `DELETE /loans/{loanId}/tax-transcript-request-forms/{taxTranscriptRequestFormId}`
- Added `PUT /loans/{loanId}/documents/{documentId}/reclassify` to reclassify a document
- Added the following objective condition endpoints:
    - `POST /loans/{loanId}/objective-conditions/{objectiveConditionId}/approve`
    - `POST /loans/{loanId}/objective-conditions/{objectiveConditionId}/cancel`
    - `POST /loans/{loanId}/objective-conditions/{objectiveConditionId}/reopen`
- Added the following pricing endpoints:
    - `POST /loans/{loanId}/pricing/clear-product-selection`
    - `POST /loans/{loanId}/pricing/deny-request`
    - `GET /loans/{loanId}/pricing/deny-request/{jobId}`
- Added the following user role endpoints to append and remove roles from a user:
    - `POST /users/{userId}/roles`
    - `DELETE /users/{userId}/roles/{roleId}`
- Added the following objective template endpoints:
    - `GET /admin-config/{processVersionId}/objective-templates`
    - `GET /admin-config/{processVersionId}/objective-templates/{templateId}`
- `GET /admin-config/roles` and `GET /admin-config/roles/{roleId}` responses now include role permission details
- Webhook endpoints can enable **batched** delivery for loan-scoped webhooks (via Vesta when configuring the endpoint): when eligible, multiple events from the same change batch are delivered in one HTTP request as a `LoanBatchWebhook` whose `events` array contains the payloads you would otherwise receive as separate deliveries.
- Added various data model fields and enum types across several entity types.

</details>
<details>
<summary style="font-size:160%;"><b>Migrating from 25.4 to 26.2</b></summary>

- Please review the breaking data model changes below.
- If you have any questions or concerns, please contact Vesta.

### Breaking changes

- Creating or patching `ClosingCost` entities to use computed fee types or computed fee categories is rejected with 400 (those fees are populated by the system instead)
- Creating `PartnerBroker` or `CorrespondentLender` loan file contacts with `IsPrimary` true is rejected with 400; assign the contact as the loan originator instead
- Sending an empty array for items without an id field now clears the existing collection instead of noop's

#### Data model changes

- `Loan`
  - Removed `TitleInsuranceProvider` (use `TitleInsuranceProvider` on the subject or owned `Property` instead)
  - Made the following fields read-only
    - `PropertyTaxEscrowedIndicator`
    - `StateTaxEscrowedIndicator`
    - `CityTaxEscrowedIndicator`
    - `VillageTaxEscrowedIndicator`
    - `SchoolTaxEscrowedIndicator`
    - `TownTaxEscrowedIndicator`
    - `HomeInsuranceEscrowedIndicator`
    - `FloodInsuranceEscrowedIndicator`
    - `EarthquakeInsuranceEscrowedIndicator`
    - `WindAndHailInsuranceEscrowedIndicator`
    - `MUDTaxEscrowedIndicator`
    - `SupplementalTaxEscrowedIndicator`
    - `SupplementalPropertyInsuranceEscrowedIndicator`
    - `FireInsuranceEscrowedIndicator`
    - `HurricaneInsuranceEscrowedIndicator`
    - `VolcanoInsuranceEscrowedIndicator`
  - Renamed `Texas12DayNoticeReceivedDate` to `InitialTexas12DayNoticeSentDate`
- `LoanProduct`
  - Made `RawPrice` read-only and added `RawPriceMetadata` which can be used instead to override the value
- `Property`
  - Made the following fields read-only
    - `PropertyValuationForm`
    - `AppraisalProvider`
    - `AppraisalOrderedDate`
    - `AppraisalScheduledDate`
    - `AppraisalInspectionCompletedDate`
    - `AppraisalReceivedDate`
    - `AppraisalDeliveredToBorrowerDate`
    - `ConditionOfAppraisal`
    - `PropertyValuationMethodType`
    - `PropertyValuationMethodTypeOtherDescription`
- `Liability`
  - Made `PayoffAmount` read-only and added `PayoffAmountMetadata` which can be used instead to override the value




- Misc changes
  - Removed the singular `BrokerRoleType` and `CorrespondentRoleType` fields from `GlobalFileContact` and `FileContact` in favor of the `BrokerRoleTypes` and `CorrespondentRoleTypes` collections
  - Made `PrimaryContacts.PartnerBroker` and `PrimaryContacts.CorrespondentLender` read-only
  - Renamed `LoanPassGetQuotesRequest`'s `DutyToServeIndicator` field to `DutyToServeProductIndicator`
  - ClosingCost's with a `FeeCategory` now require a `FeeType` when adding and can not be changed when updating
  - Renamed `Borrower8821OrderStatus`'s `Failed` enum option to `ActionNeeded`
  - Renamed `ApplyEstimationResponse`'s `SmartFeesPropertyTaxEstimations` field to `IntegrationPropertyTaxEstimations`
  - Renamed `ApplyEstimationUpdate`'s `SmartFeesPropertyTaxEstimationId` field to `IntegrationPropertyTaxEstimationId`
  - Renamed `EstimatedExpensesResponse`'s `SmartFeesPropertyTaxEstimations` field to `IntegrationPropertyTaxEstimations`
  - Changed `OptimalBlueGetQuotesRequest` and `LoanPassGetQuotesRequest`'s `PricingCustomFieldInputs` fields from dictionaries to lists
  - Renamed `SmartFeesPaymentFrequency` enum to `PropertyTaxPaymentFrequency`
  - Renamed `SmartFeesPropertyTaxType` enum to `PropertyTaxType`
  - Renamed `AutomatedActionStatus`'s `Cancelled` enum option to `AutomaticallyCancelled`
  - Renamed `AutomatedActionType`'s `ArchiveDocument`, `Submit4506C`, `Update4506C`, and `Cancel4506C` enum options to `UpdateDocumentStatus`, `SubmitTaxTranscriptRequest`, `UpdateTaxTranscriptRequest`, and `CancelTaxTranscriptRequest` respectively
  - Renamed `AdminCategory`'s `FundingAndSecondary` enum option to just `Funding` and added a separate `Secondary` option
  - Removed `DocumentType` and `DocumentTypeFull`'s `AutomaticDataExtractionEnabled` fields
  - Changed `FemaDisasterCounty`'s `PlaceCode` field to the list field `PlaceCodes`



#### Webhook changes

- Removed `LoanFactChangedWebhook` as it's no longer used as they're incorporated in `DataFieldWebhook`s


### Non-breaking changes

- Added the following loan assigned-users endpoints:
    - `POST /loans/{loanId}/assigned-users`
    - `GET /loans/{loanId}/assigned-users`
- Added the following tax transcript request form endpoints:
    - `POST /loans/{loanId}/tax-transcript-request-forms`
    - `GET /loans/{loanId}/tax-transcript-request-forms`
    - `GET /loans/{loanId}/tax-transcript-request-forms/{taxTranscriptRequestFormId}`
    - `PATCH /loans/{loanId}/tax-transcript-request-forms/{taxTranscriptRequestFormId}`
    - `DELETE /loans/{loanId}/tax-transcript-request-forms/{taxTranscriptRequestFormId}`
- Added `PUT /loans/{loanId}/documents/{documentId}/reclassify` to reclassify a document
- Added the following objective condition endpoints:
    - `POST /loans/{loanId}/objective-conditions/{objectiveConditionId}/approve`
    - `POST /loans/{loanId}/objective-conditions/{objectiveConditionId}/cancel`
    - `POST /loans/{loanId}/objective-conditions/{objectiveConditionId}/reopen`
- Added the following pricing endpoints:
    - `POST /loans/{loanId}/pricing/clear-product-selection`
    - `POST /loans/{loanId}/pricing/deny-request`
    - `GET /loans/{loanId}/pricing/deny-request/{jobId}`
- Added the following user role endpoints to append and remove roles from a user:
    - `POST /users/{userId}/roles`
    - `DELETE /users/{userId}/roles/{roleId}`
- Added the following objective template endpoints:
    - `GET /admin-config/{processVersionId}/objective-templates`
    - `GET /admin-config/{processVersionId}/objective-templates/{templateId}`
- `GET /admin-config/roles` and `GET /admin-config/roles/{roleId}` responses now include role permission details
- Webhook endpoints can enable **batched** delivery for loan-scoped webhooks (via Vesta when configuring the endpoint): when eligible, multiple events from the same change batch are delivered in one HTTP request as a `LoanBatchWebhook` whose `events` array contains the payloads you would otherwise receive as separate deliveries.
- Added the `GET /admin-config` endpoint to retrieve process version information
- Added the `PUT /loans/{loanId}/objective-tasks/{taskId}/external-message` endpoint to set the external message override on a document required objective task
- Added the following admin configuration branches endpoints:
    - `POST /admin-config/branches`
    - `PATCH /admin-config/branches/{branchId}`
- Added the following closing scheduling endpoints:
    - `GET /loans/{loanId}/closing-scheduling/available-providers`
    - `POST /loans/{loanId}/closing-scheduling`
- Added the following objectives endpoints:
    - `PUT /loans/{loanId}/objectives/{objectiveId}/deescalate`
    - `PUT /loans/{loanId}/objectives/{objectiveId}/unblock`
- Added various data model fields and enum types across several entity types.

</details>

post.description:
Uploads a document with files. The Content-Type must be multipart/form-data.
The first part of the request should be JSON document metadata, containing the DocumentUploadMetadata type (see types section below).
Remaining parts should be files, with Content-Type set. Only PDFs, PNGs, and JPEGs are supported.
The maximum size of all files and metadata is 100MB.
Note: while the schema indicates that request parts can be sent in any order, the document metadata must be sent first.

body.description: The first section must be JSON containing the DocumentUploadMetadata type (see types section below).

filename.description: List of files being uploaded through multipart/form-data.

200.description: Returns the ID of the created document.

400.description: Invalid document type specified, invalid associated entity, no files were provided, or input is not well-formed.

post.description:
Converts MISMO 3.4 XML to a JSON body suitable for creating or modifying a loan. The MISMO XML should be uploaded with Content-Type 'application/xml' or 'text/xml'.

parameters.description: If true, the loan will not synchronously calculate computed fields or triggers before returning.

parameters.description: Optional prefix to prepend to the loan number. This overrides any branch code prefix. Must be a positive number up to 8 digits.

requestBody.description:
The full loan data to be created. You can specify entities to be created, and specify entity relationships
by using "ref" fields as mentioned in the documentation above. See the schema for more information.

200.description: Returns the ID of the created loan and the IDs of any entities with specified ref fields.

userId.description: The userId to add to the loan's assigned users.

userIds.description: The full list of userIds currently assigned to the loan (existing + newly added).

addressValidEnd.description: The end date of the borrower's residency at the address, if applicable.

addressValidStart.description: The start date of the borrower's residency at the address, if applicable.

companyName.description: The company name at the address.

country.description: The country of the address. Not needed for US addresses.

fipsCode.description: The FIPS code of the address as determined from the state code and county.

internationalIndicator.description: Indicates whether the address is international.

isVerified.description: Legacy field indicating whether the address has been verified against the USPS database.

landlordEmailAddress.description: The email address of the landlord of the property, if applicable.

landlordFullName.description: The full name of the landlord of the property, if applicable.

landlordPhoneNumber.description: The phone number of the landlord of the property, if applicable.

line.description: The street address. This is the first line of the address excluding apartment or unit numbers.

line2.description: The second line of the address. Usually used for apartment numbers or unit numbers.

monthlyRentAmount.description: The monthly rent paid by the borrower, if applicable.

numberOfUnits.description: Number of units in the address

occupancyType.description: The borrower's type of occupancy of the property (own, rent, or live rent free), if applicable.

postalCode.description: The postal code of the address, if applicable. Used for international addresses.

province.description: The province of the address, if applicable.

state.description: The state name of the address (e.g. California, New York, etc.)

stateCode.description: The state code of the address (e.g. CA, NY, etc.)

status.description: Indicates whether the address is the current or previous address of the borrower.

types.description: The type of address - mailing, residential, or other.

zipCode.description: The ZIP code of the address. Can be 5 or 9 digits.

roleIds.description: The IDs of the roles to add to the user.

fnmNeighborsMortgageEligibilityIndicator.description: Indicates if the loan is eligible for the Fannie Mae Neighbors program.

hudIncomeLimitAdjustment.description: The percentage by which the HUD Median Income can be exceeded in certain high cost areas.

hudLendingIncomeLimit.description: The total dollar amount for HUD lending based on the median income and adjustment factors.

hudMedianIncomeAmount.description: The HUD estimated median family incomes to determine borrower eligibility for all applications related to affordable lending products.

AgentTaskRun.description:
External representation of a single AI agent run on an objective task.
One objective task can have multiple agent runs over its lifetime.

id.description: Unique ID of the agent run.

loanId.description: ID of the loan the agent run was performed on.

loanNumber.description: Loan number of the loan the agent run was performed on.

objectiveId.description: ID of the objective the task belongs to.

objectiveTemplateExternalIdentifier.description: External identifier of the objective template, if one is configured.

objectiveTemplateName.description: Name of the objective template the objective was created from.

reviews.description:
Reviews (e.g. thumbs up/down) submitted on the task by lender users. Reviews are scoped to the task,
so all runs of the same task will share the same set of reviews.

status.description: Outcome status of the agent run. Values include Completed, UnableToComplete, Blocked, Escalated, Error, Unknown.

taskEntityId.description: ID of the entity the task is scoped to.

taskEntityType.description: Type of the entity the task is scoped to.

taskId.description: ID of the objective task the agent run was performed on.

taskTemplateExternalIdentifier.description: External identifier of the task template, if one is configured.

taskTemplateName.description: Display name of the task template.

taskTemplateType.description: Type of the task template (e.g. Instructions, DocumentRequired, FormInput).

isPositive.description: True if the reviewer marked the agent run as positive ("thumbs up"); false if negative ("thumbs down").

reviewedAt.description: Time at which the review was submitted.

reviewerName.description: Display name of the user who submitted the review.

reviewerUserId.description: ID of the user who submitted the review.

reviewNotes.description: Free-text notes provided by the reviewer.

$action.description: Only used for adding and removing items from a collection

alternativeLoanOptionLender.description: Name of the financial institution providing the loan

alternativeLoanOptionLoanAmortizationTyp.description: Structure determining how principal and interest are paid over time

alternativeLoanOptionLoanDiscountPoints.description: Upfront payment to reduce the loan's interest rate, expressed as a percentage of the loan amount

alternativeLoanOptionLoanFeatures.description: Special characteristics or provisions that affect the loan's terms or repayment structure

alternativeLoanOptionLoanOptionType.description: The alternative loan option type based on the standard CFPB defined alternative loan options

alternativeLoanOptionLoanOriginationFee.description: Upfront fee charged by the lender or broker

alternativeLoanOptionLoanTermMonthsCount.description: Total duration of the loan expressed in months (e.g. 360 for a 30 year loan)

alternativeLoanOptionNoteRate.description: Annual interest rate stated on the mortgage note

alternativeLoanOptionPrepaymentPenaltyAm.description: Fee charged for prepayment of principal

alternativeLoanOptionProgramName.description: Product or program name used by the lender for this loan

originalReasonForFlagging.description: Original reason for flagging from the previous appraisal review

previousIssueStatus.description: Status indicating whether the issue appeared in a previous appraisal review

ssrCode.description: The SSR code for the issue

ssrCodeDescription.description: Description of the SSR code issue

$action.description: Only used for adding and removing items from a collection

accountIdentifier.description: Unique identifier if the asset is an account, typically last 4 digits of account number

amountAllocatedTowardDownPayment.description: The portion of this asset that is allocated toward the down payment for the loan.

amountUsedTowardsClosing.description: The amount this asset will contribute to the cash to close the loan

assetDescription.description: Description of asset, most often used for stocks/bonds

assetType.description: A value from a MISMO prescribed list that specifies financial assets in a mortgage loan transaction.

assetValue.description: The value of the asset, regardless of liquidity

assetVerificationCurrentAsOfDatetime.description: The date that the asset verification report is current as of.

assetVerificationOrders.description: The asset verification orders placed on this asset

assetVerifications.description: The asset verifications that relate to this asset

assetVerificationStatus.description: Indicates the overall status of this particular asset's verification. If the asset was ever verified, this will always show completed.

assetVerifiedDatetime.description: Indicates the datetime that the asset verification was successfully verified.

associatedBusiness.description: This is a reference to the borrower-owned business that holds thsi asset, if it is a business asset

associatedProperty.description: This is a reference to the owned property that will generate the net sale proceeds for this asset

borrowers.description: This is an array of references to the borrowers who are account holders on this asset

capitalGainsIncome.description: The capital gains income that is associated with this account, if applicable

collateralizedLoanAmount.description: The total outstanding balance of loans collateralized by this account.

earnestMoneyDepositAmount.description: The amount of the earnest money deposit that comes from this depository account, if applicable.

earnestMoneyHasClearedAccountIndicator.description: Indicates whether the earnest money deposit has cleared the account balance. If false, we deduct the earnest money amount from the qualified value for this asset.

earnestMoneySourceIndicator.description: Indicates whether this asset is a source of earnest money

fannieMaeAssetRepresentationAndWarrantyR.description: Validated amount returned by DU for this asset under Fannie Mae asset representation and warranty relief.
Validation status returned by DU for this asset under Fannie Mae asset representation and warranty relief.

gifterOrGranterAddress.description: The address of the person providing the gift

gifterOrGranterEIN.description: The employee identification number of the person or entity providing the gift or grant

gifterOrGranterName.description: Full name of the person providing the gift

gifterOrGranterPhoneNumber.description: The phone number of the person providing the gift

giftOrGrantDateOfTransfer.description: The date the gift funds were transferred into the borrower's account

giftOrGrantDepositedToSettlementAgent.description: Indicates whether the gift funds have been deposited to the settlement agent

giftOrGrantIncludedInAssetAccount.description: Indicates whether the gift funds have already been included as part of an asset account considered for the loan

giftOrGrantIsSellerFunded.description: Indicates whether the gift is funded by the seller

giftOrGrantSource.description: Relationship of the gift source to the borrower

hasLargeDeposits.description: Are there any large deposits in notable activities

hasOverdrafts.description: Are there any overdrafts in notable activities

institutionEmailAddress.description: The best email address to contact the institution

institutionName.description: Financial institution holding the funding source, typically if it is an account of some kind

institutionPhoneNumber.description: The best phone number to contact the institution

interestAndDividendsIncome.description: The interest and dividends income that is associated with this account, if applicable

isLiquidAsset.description: Indicates if the asset is liquid.

nonBorrowerAccountHolderNames.description: This is an array of full names of account holders who are not on the subject loan

notableActivities.description: List of large deposits and NSFs/overdrafts that have been observed in the account and need to be explained

numberOfOptions.description: The number of vested stock options

otherAssetType.description: Further description of the asset type if it is not in the prescribed list

otherGiftOrGrantSource.description: Further description of the gifter's relationship if not in the list of gift sources

penaltiesOnDistributionOfFunds.description: The total penalties that would be incurred on the distribution of these funds used for asset-based income.

retirementFundType.description: Specifies the type of retirement account, such as IRA, SEP, Keogh, or 401(k).

retirementIncome.description: The retirement income that is associated with this account, if applicable

saleOfRealEstateListingPrice.description: The listing price for the property that will be sold.

stockOptionCurrentPrice.description: The current price per share of the stock underlying these vested stock options

stockOptionExercisePrice.description: The price per share at which these vested stock options can be exercised into the underlying stock

trusteeName.description: The legal name of the trustee for a trust account asset

trustIncome.description: Income associated with this trust account, if applicable

usableFundsAmount.description: Funds usable for this loan transaction, used for underwriting.

accountNumber.description: The verified account number that was returned from the asset verification order.

accountType.description: The account type that was returned from the asset verification order.

assetValue.description: The verified asset value that was returned from the asset verification order.

currentAsOfDatetime.description: Indicates the datetime that the asset verification is current as of.

externalVerificationId.description: The external verification ID that was returned from the asset verification order.

financialInstitute.description: The verified financial institute that was returned from the asset verification order.

verifiedDateTime.description: Indicates the datetime that the asset verification was successfully verified.

associatedAssetId.description: Indicates the asset that the reverify order was placed for

orderedDateTime.description: The date that the asset verification order was placed

orderStatus.description: The current status of the asset verification order that was placed for a particular borrower

provider.description: The asset verification provider that the order was placed with

requestType.description: Whether the request that was placed was the initial asset verification order or a reverify

agencyCaseNumber.description: The agency case number for the assumed loan

amortizationType.description: The amortization type of the assumed loan

annualGovernmentLoanMortgageInsuranceRat.description: The annual government loan mortgage insurance rate

assumptionReason.description: The reason for the assumption of the loan

assumptionReasonOtherDescription.description: Description when assumption reason is Other

currentMonthlyPaymentAmount.description: The current total monthly payment amount on the assumed loan

currentRate.description: The current interest rate on the assumed loan

debtToIncome.description: The debt-to-income ratio for the assumed loan

financedMIP.description: The financed mortgage insurance premium amount

firstPaymentDate.description: The first payment date of the assumed loan

fromSameLenderIndicator.description: Indicates whether the assumed loan is from the same lender

lenderLoanNumber.description: The lender's loan number for the assumed loan

lienPriorityType.description: The lien priority of the assumed loan

loanTerm.description: The original term of the assumed loan in months

loanToValue.description: The loan-to-value ratio for the assumed loan

mortgageCurrentHolder.description: The current holder of the assumed mortgage

mortgageType.description: The mortgage type of the assumed loan

noteAmount.description: The original note amount of the assumed loan

noteDate.description: The date of the note for the assumed loan

noteRate.description: The original note rate of the assumed loan

principalAndInterestMonthlyPayment.description: The principal and interest portion of the monthly payment

programName.description: The program name of the assumed loan

remainingTermNumberOfMonths.description: The remaining term of the assumed loan in months

$action.description: Only used for adding and removing items from a collection

aus.description: The automated underwriting system used to generate the result.

caseIdentifier.description: The unique identifier for the case run provided by the automated underwriting system.

gseDutyToServe.description: Indicates whether the loan qualifies under the Duty to Serve mandate for Fannie and Freddie.

gseMissionScore.description: The mission score provided by the automated underwriting system.

initiatedAt.description: The date and time the run of the automated underwriting system was initiated.

isExternal.description: Indicates whether the automated underwriting response was generated externally.

otherResult.description: The result provided by the automated underwriting system if it is not one of the predefined results.

result.description: The result providded by the automated underwriting system.

bankruptcyCaseNumber.description: The court-issued case number associated with the bankruptcy filing being reported.

bankruptcyChapterType.description: The chapter under which the borrower filed their most recent bankruptcy

bankruptcyCloseDate.description: The date on which the bankruptcy case was closed

bankruptcyClosedType.description: Whether the borrower's most recent bankruptcy was discharged or dismissed

bankruptcyDischargedMortgageObligationIn.description: Whether the borrower's bankruptcy discharged a mortgage obligation

$action.description: Only used for adding and removing items from a collection

activityType.description: The type of activity for this transaction

amount.description: Amount debited or credited in the transaction. This should always be a positive value

date.description: Date that the transaction was posted

description.description: The transaction description as provided by the financial institution

sourcedAmount.description: The sourced portion of a large deposit.

unsourcedAmount.description: The unsourced portion of a large deposit.

blockedByTaskIdsUntilComplete.description: The IDs of the tasks that will block the objective until they are completed if Reason is type BlockUntilTaskCompletion

blockingUserId.description: The user who is blocking the objective.

otherReason.description: The reason for blocking the objective.

reasonId.description:
The ID of the reason for blocking the objective.
This ID can be retrieved from the admin-config endpoints with includes option `objectiveBlockedAndEscalatedReasons`

userSelectedActionType.description: The user-selected action type for UserSelect block reasons.

userSelectedBlockedForSpecificDateTime.description: The date and time to block the objective for.

userSelectedBlockedForSpecificHours.description: The number of hours to block the objective for.

$action.description: Only used for adding and removing items from a collection
Only used for adding and removing items from a collection

addresses.description: The borrower's previous residential addresses, with at least two years of history expected

ageAsOfClosing.description: The borrower's age as of the closing date on the loan

aliases.description: A list of all of the other full names a borrower might go by/that might appear on other documents

applicationSubmitDate.description: The date on which the borrower submitted their application

applicationSubmitMethod.description: How the borrower submitted their application (online, phone, etc)

assetVerificationOrders.description: The asset verification orders that have been placed for this borrower

assumedMortgageOriginalBorrowerIndicator.description: Indicates whether this borrower was an original borrower on the assumed mortgage

bankruptcies.description: A list of the borrower's bankruptcy cases.

bankruptcyCount.description: The number of bankruptcies the borrower has declared in the last 7 years

bankruptcyIndicator.description: Whether the borrower declares they declared bankruptcy in the last 7 years

bankruptcyLatestCloseDate.description: The date on which the borrower's most recent bankruptcy case was closed

borrowerHasCoBorrowingSpouse.description: Indicates whether the borrower has a spouse who is also a borrower on the loan

borrowerOnPreviousMortgageIndicator.description: Indicates whether this borrower was a borrower on the previous mortgage that is being refinanced.

caivrsIdentifier.description: The borrower's unique identifier in HUD's CAIVRS system

citizenshipType.description: The borrower's citizenship status with respect to the United States

countryOfCitizenship.description: The borrower's country of citizenship, if they are not a citizen of the United States

creditConsentOptionType.description: The type of credit pull the borrower has consented to, hard or soft

creditProvider.description: The external credit provider used to pull the borrower's credit report

creditPulledDate.description: The date on which the borrower's credit was pulled

creditPullReportType.description: Credit pull report type -- Merge, MergePlus, etc.

creditPullStatus.description: The current status of the most recent credit pull report

creditReferenceNumber.description: Reference number for the credit report, used to refresh the report, provided to DU/LPA, etc

creditRefreshPulledDate.description: The date on which the borrower's credit refresh was pulled

creditRefreshReferenceNumber.description: Reference number for the credit refresh report

creditRefreshReportIssuedDate.description: The date the credit refresh report was issued, updated only with a new credit refresh pull but not with retrievals

creditRefreshRequestType.description: Credit refresh request type - joint or individual

creditRefreshStatus.description: The current status of the most recent credit refresh pull report

creditReportIssuedDate.description: The date the credit report was issued, updated only with a new credit pull but not with retrievals or upgrades.

creditReportType.description: Credit request type - joint or individual

creditScores.description: List of the credit scores provided by the various bureaus on the borrower

debtMonitoringCancelDate.description: The date on which the borrower's UDM was cancelled

debtMonitoringLastUpdatedAt.description: The date on which the borrower's UDM doc was last queried

debtMonitoringOrderedDate.description: The date on which the undisclosed debt monitoring order was initiated. This may not match the start date, because at order, a start date in the past can be selected

debtMonitoringReferenceNumber.description: Reference number for the debt monitoring request, used to retrieve updates on the report

debtMonitoringStartDate.description: The date from which any borrower credit activity will be monitored

debtMonitoringStatus.description: Whether or not UDM is currently enabled for a borrower

declaredNoIncomeIndicator.description: Indicates that the borrower has explicitly declared no income for the loan. When set, this will still trigger TRID even if no income is entered

deedInLieuLatestCompletionDate.description: If the borrower conveyed a deed in lieu of foreclosure in the last seven years, the date on which the deed in lieu was completed

dependentAges.description: A list of the ages of the borrower's dependents

derogatoryEventsExtenuatingCircumstances.description: Whether there were extenuating circumstances surrounding the derogatory events

disabilityIndicator.description: Indicates whether the borrower has a disability. Used in the USDA adjusted annual income calculation to determine eligibility for the elderly/disabled family deduction and the medical expense deduction.

domesticRelationshipIndicator.description: Whether the borrower is in a domestic relationship

domesticRelationshipStateCode.description: The state that recognizes the borrower's domestic relationship status

domesticRelationshipStatus.description: The borrower's domestic relationship status, if recognized by the state

dudoCreditOverrides.description: Codes to tell DU/DO to disregard the derogatory credit event from the DU/DO eligibility assessment

econsentCaptureMethod.description: Method in which the econsent was given (electronic, verbal)

econsentIpAddress.description: IP address that was used to econsent

econsentStatus.description: The status of the econsent (accept, decline, withdrawn)

employmentAuthorizationDocumentCode.description: The employment authorization document code under which the borrower is currently working in the United States

estimatedFederalTaxes.description: Estimated federal tax amount for the borrower

estimatedRetirementDeductions.description: Estimated retirement deduction amount for the borrower

estimatedStateTaxes.description: Estimated state tax amount for the borrower

excessPurchasePriceAwarenessType.description: Indicates when the borrower became aware that the appraised value is less than the purchase price.

fannieMaeIncomeRepresentationAndWarranty.description: The borrower-level Fannie Mae income representation and warranty relief total validated amount.

fhaLimitedDenialParticipationIndicator.description: Indicates whether the borrower exists on either the LDP or SAM exclusionary lists

filedPreDischargeClaimIndicator.description: Indicates whether the borrower has filed a pre-discharge claim with the VA.

financialInterestInSubjectPropertyRental.description: Whether the borrower has financial interest in the subject property's rental units.
Whether the borrower has financial interest in the subject property's rental units.

firstTimeHomebuyerIndicator.description: Whether the borrower is a first time homebuyer

firstTimeVAEntitlementIndicator.description: Indicates whether this is the borrower's first time using their VA entitlement

fiveYearForeclosureExplanation.description: Explanation for indication foreclosure or transfer in past five years

foreclosureLatestCompletionDate.description: If the borrower was foreclosed on in the last seven years, the date on which the foreclosure was completed

freddieMacIncomeRepresentationAndWarrant.description: The borrower-level Freddie Mac income representation and warranty relief total validated amount.

hardCreditConsentDate.description: Indicates that the borrower has provided consent to have their hard credit pulled, and the date on which they consented. This field blocks hard credit pulls if not set

hardCreditConsentType.description: What type of consent the borrower has provided to have their hard credit pulled

hasAtLeastOneYearHistoryOfInvestmentProp.description: Indicates whether the borrower has at least one year history of investment property management

hmdaEthnicityDetails.description: The borrower's self reported ethnicity information for HMDA purposes

hmdaGenderDetails.description: The borrower's self reported gender information for HMDA purposes

hmdaRaceDetails.description: The borrower's self reported race information for HMDA purposes

homeownerPastThreeYears.description: Whether the borrower declares they have owned a home in the past 3 years

housingCounselingSessions.description: Details pertaining to the borrower's completion of housing counseling, should that be necessary. Includes the counseling agency details, date and method of completion, etc

hudFhaRealEstatePastSixtyMonthsIndicator.description: Whether the borrower owned or sold other real estate in the last 5 years with a HUD/FHA mortgage.

incomeVerificationConsentDate.description: Indicates that the borrower has provided consent to have their income verified, and the date on which they consented.

incomeVerificationConsentType.description: What type of consent the borrower has provided to have their income verified

intentToOccupy.description: Whether the borrower declares they will be occupying the property

irsAuthorizationOr8821Status.description: The status of the IRS account authorization or Form 8821 processing for tax transcript orders

isUsingVABenefitsIndicator.description: Determines whether the borrower is using their VA benefits

languagePreferenceType.description: If the borrower prefers a language other than English, the preferred language for the borrower

latestAssetVerificationURL.description: The URL sent to the borrower for asset verification.

latestIncomeVerificationURL.description: The URL last sent to the borrower for income verification.

ldpgsaCheckDate.description: Date that the lender checked this borrower against the limited denial of participation and general services administration lists

loanAmountAdjustmentConsentDate.description: Indicates that the borrower has provided consent to allow the loan amount to be adjusted throughout the loan process, and the date on which they consented.

loanAmountAdjustmentConsentType.description: What type of consent the borrower has provided to allow the loan amount to be adjusted throughout the loan process

lpaCreditReportVendorIdentifierOverride.description: Override field for specifying the LPA credit report vendor identifier that credit was originally pulled with. Only used for wholesale loans.

medianCreditScore.description: The median credit score of the borrower

militaryDischargeType.description: If the borrower is a veteran, this describes the nature of their discharge

militaryServiceExpectedCompletionDate.description: If the borrower is on active duty, this is the date their service is expected to end

militaryStatusType.description: Describes the borrower's military service, if applicable. If the borrower is a spouse eligible for VA benefits, this field will describe their spouse's military service instead.

monthlyFederalTaxAmount.description: Estimated monthly taxes for this borrower

mortgageChargeOffLatestCompletionDate.description: If the borrower had a mortgage charged off in the last seven years, the date on which this occurred

mostRecentTradelineOpenDate.description: The most recent date on which any of the borrower's tradeline accounts was opened

nameChangedSinceLastFiledTaxIndicator.description: Indicates whether a borrower's name has changed since they last filed tax returns.

nearestLivingRelativeAddress.description: Address of the veteran's nearest living relative

nearestLivingRelativeFirstName.description: First name of the veteran's nearest living relative

nearestLivingRelativeLastName.description: Last name of the veteran's nearest living relative

nearestLivingRelativeMiddleName.description: Middle name of the veteran's nearest living relative

nearestLivingRelativePhoneNumber.description: Phone number of the veteran's nearest living relative

nearestLivingRelativeRelationship.description: Relationship of the nearest living relative to the veteran

nearestLivingRelativeSuffixName.description: Suffix of the veteran's nearest living relative

nickname.description: A nickname the borrower uses in place of their full name, for communication with the borrower

occupyingPersons.description: List of family members (spouse or dependents) who occupied the property while the borrower was on active duty

openTradelines.description: Number indicating the count of tradelines

otherDeductions.description: Other deduction amounts for the borrower not included in federal taxes, state taxes, or retirement deductions

otherDomesticRelationshipStatus.description: Further detail about the borrower's domestic relationship status, if not in the prescribed dropdown

otherLanguagePreference.description: If the borrower prefers a language other than English and that is not in the prescribed dropdown, the preferred language for the borrower

otherLoanNumbersSharingThisBorrower.description: A list of loan numbers that share the same borrower.

outstandingJudgmentsIndicator.description: Whether the borrower declares they are subject to outstanding judgements

partyToLawsuitIndicator.description: Whether the borrower declares they are currently party to a lawsuit

phoneNumbers.description: A list of the borrower's phone numbers and their types (mobile, home, etc)

positiveRentalHistoryIndicator.description: Whether the borrower has on-time payment of all rental payments in the previous 12 months.

powerOfAttorneyAgentFirstName.description: The first name of the borrower's agent for closing

powerOfAttorneyAgentLastName.description: The last name of the borrower's agent for closing

powerOfAttorneyAgentMiddleName.description: The middle name of the borrower's agent for closing

powerOfAttorneyApprovedIndicator.description: Indicates the lender has reviewed and approved the power of attorney for closing

powerOfAttorneySignatureText.description: The text that appears in the signature line for the borrower's power of attorney agent

preferredContactMethod.description: The borrower indicated preferred contact method

prefersNonEnglishLanguageIndicator.description: Indicates that the borrower prefers to communciate about this transaction in a language other than English

presentlyDelinquentIndicator.description: Whether the borrower declares they are presently delinquent on a federal debt

previousHudFhaPropertyOriginalMortgageAm.description: Original mortgage amount of previous HUD/FHA-financed property.

previousHudFhaPropertyOwnedIndicator.description: Whether the borrower still owns their previous HUD/FHA-financed property.

previousHudFhaPropertySalePrice.description: Sale price of previous HUD/FHA-financed property.

previouslyOccupiedIndicator.description: For VA IRRRL loans, whether this borrower previously occupied the subject propery

previouslyOccupyingPersons.description: List of family members (spouse or dependents) who previously occupied the property while the borrower was on active duty

previousVALoans.description: Collection of previous VA loans that the borrower has obtained, including information about associated liens and property ownership status.

priorMortgageChargeOffIndicator.description: Whether the borrower declares they had a mortgage charged off in the last 7 years

priorPropertyDeedInLieuConveyedIndicator.description: Whether the borrower declares they have conveyed a deed in lieu of foreclosure in the last 7 years

priorPropertyForeclosureCompletedIndicat.description: Whether the borrower declares they completed foreclosure in the last 7 years

priorPropertyShortSaleCompletedIndicator.description: Whether the borrower declares they completed a short sale in the last 7 years

priorPropertyTitleType.description: If the borrower indicated they owned a home in the past 3 years, how they held title to that property

priorPropertyUsageType.description: If the borrower indicated they owned a home in the past 3 years, how they used that property

shortSaleLatestCompletionDate.description: If the borrower completed a short sale in the last seven years, the date on which the short sale was completed

softCreditConsentType.description: What type of consent the borrower has provided to have their soft credit pulled

specialBorrowerSellerRelationshipIndicat.description: Whether the borrower declares they have a special relationship with the seller of the property in a purchase

spouse.description: A reference to the borrower's spouse if the spouse is also on the loan

spouseEligibleForVABenefits.description: Indicates whether the borrower is receiving VA surviving spouse benefits

spouseFullNameIfNonBorrower.description: The full name of the borrower's spouse if that person is not on the mortgage application

ssnVerificationOrderedDate.description: The date on which the SSN verification report was ordered

ssnVerificationReportIdentifier.description: The identifier for the SSN verification report

ssnVerificationReportProvider.description: The provider of the SSN verification report

ssnVerificationStatus.description: The status of the SSN verification report

suffixName.description: The borrower's legal suffix name, if applicable

taxIdentifier.description: The borrower's tax identifier number, most typically an SSN

taxIdentifierType.description: What type of tax identifier number the borrower has

taxReturnLastFiledFirstName.description: The borrower's first name on their last filed tax return (when different from the borrower's name on the loan).

taxReturnLastFiledLastName.description: The borrower's last name on their last filed tax return (when different from the borrower's name on the loan).

taxReturnLastFiledMiddleName.description: The borrower's middle name on their last filed tax return (when different from the borrower's name on the loan).

tcpaConsentDate.description: Indicates when the borrower provided TCPA consent to be contacted via phone calls or text messages.

tcpaConsentType.description: What type of consent the borrower has provided under the Telephone Consumer Protection Act (TCPA) to allow the lender to contact them via phone calls or text messages

undisclosedBorrowedFundsAmount.description: If the borrower indicated the use of other undisclosed borrowed funds, the amount of those funds

undisclosedMortgageApplicationIndicator.description: Whether the borrower declares they will apply for another mortgage before closing

unmatchedAssetVerificationsIndicator.description: Indicates whether a particular borrower has any asset verifications that are unmatched from the existing assets on the loan.

path.description:
Path is set for non many-to-many relationships, and indicates the specific relationship that was changed (as a JSONPath string).
When Path is set, Entity1 is the entity that has the field, and Entity2 is the entity that is being linked.
In a one:many relationship, Entity1 is the child (many) entity, and Entity2 is the parent (one) entity.

distributionsHistory.description: Distributions for this self-employment income (K-1 Box 16 for S Corp, K-1 Line 19 for Partnership)

fairRentalDays.description: The number of days in the previous year that the property was occupied and rented. (IRS 1040 Schedule E, Line 2

fairRentalDaysHistory.description: The number of days in the given year that the property was occupied and rented. (IRS 1040 Schedule E, Line 2)
